headline: Spring Equinox, Fresh Design Energy
subheadline: AI agents are getting design literacy, Figma ships big updates, and a fascinating new take on vibe coding workflows.

sections.label: Big Figma News
sections.title: Figma Introduces Agent-Ready API Endpoints for Design Systems
sections.description: Figma just expanded their Dev Mode API with endpoints purpose-built for AI agent consumption — structured token output, component metadata queries, and variant resolution. This is a massive unlock for your MCP integration work, essentially giving agents first-class access to design system semantics rather than pixel scraping.

sections.label: Deep Dive
sections.title: The Model Context Protocol Is Becoming the USB-C of AI Tooling
sections.description: A thorough analysis of how MCP adoption has accelerated in Q1 2026, with Anthropic's protocol now supported by 40+ tools. The piece highlights design tooling as one of the fastest-growing MCP connector categories — validating the exact space you're building in. Includes interviews with teams building Figma and Sketch connectors.

sections.label: Must Watch
sections.title: Vibe Coding 2.0: When AI Understands Your Design Tokens
sections.description: A brilliant walkthrough showing a Cursor + Claude workflow where design tokens from Figma automatically flow into code generation via MCP. The creator demonstrates building a full component library in 20 minutes. Directly relevant to what you're building — and shows the demand is real.

sections.label: Mental Model
sections.title: Design Systems as Agent Operating Systems
sections.description: Emerging thinking from several design leaders: your design system isn't just documentation for humans anymore — it's the 'OS' that AI agents use to make aesthetic and structural decisions. Token hierarchies, component APIs, and usage guidelines become the instruction set. This reframes design system work as infrastructure for the AI-native product era.

sections.label: New Tool
sections.title: Lovable v3 Launches with Multi-Agent Collaboration Mode
sections.description: Lovable's new release adds a multi-agent canvas where a 'designer agent' and 'engineer agent' iterate together on UI. It now imports Figma components directly and reasons about layout intent. Worth testing to see how it compares to your own MCP approach — and potentially useful as a rapid prototyping layer.

sections.label: Nap-Time Listen
sections.title: From IC to Leader and Back: Non-Linear Design Careers
sections.description: A refreshingly honest conversation about senior designers who step away (for leave, burnout, or curiosity) and return with sharper instincts. Guests include a former Spotify design director who used her leave to build indie tools. Feels very relevant to your current chapter — building your own thing while navigating the identity shift.

sections.label: Worth Knowing
sections.title: 67% of design teams now use AI in their daily workflow
sections.description: Up from 34% a year ago. But here's the kicker: only 11% report using AI that's connected to their actual design system. The gap between 'using AI' and 'using AI that understands our design language' is enormous — and it's exactly the gap your Figma MCP work addresses.
